item.title: Hyundai Accelerates US Output to Lighten Profit Hit From Tariffs
item.description: Hyundai Motor Co. plans to ramp up US production and cut expenses to shield the South Korean carmaker from tariffs that are eating into profit in its biggest market.

item.title: Braskem Majority Stake Sale by Novonor Advances With Signed Deal
item.description: The sale of a controlling stake in Brazilian petrochemicals producer Braskem SA moved forward another step after troubled conglomerate Novonor signed an agreement to sell its shares to a fund backed by asset manager IG4 Capital.

item.title: DOJ Steps Up Agriculture Market Scrutiny Amid Rising Prices
item.description: As millions of Americans face an affordability crisis, the Justice Department is homing in on the agriculture industry, focusing on the rising costs faced by farmers and ranchers and soaring prices of key grocery staples like beef and eggs.

item.title: Bank of Canada Surveys Show War Hitting Inflation Expectations
item.description: The war in Iran is pushing up inflation expectations, after sentiment had been improving prior to the conflict, survey data released Monday by the Bank of Canada shows.

item.title: SEC, CFTC Propose Narrowing Hedge Fund Reporting Requirements
item.description: Wall Street’s top regulators unveiled a plan to reduce hedge fund reporting requirements, according to a statement.

item.title: Eurizon’s Jen Sees Chinese Yuan Rallying by Record 9% This Year
item.description: China may permit record yuan appreciation this year as it tries to boost confidence in its currency and enhance local companies’ purchasing power overseas, according to Stephen Jen, chief investment officer at Eurizon SLJ Capital.

item.title: Optiver Steps Toward AI, Crypto Startup Exposure with VC Stake
item.description: Optiver Holding BV has taken an equity stake in crypto and artificial intelligence-focused venture capital firm Eden Block, as the two technologies continue to attract institutional backing.

item.title: Italy Eyes Deficit Under 3% of GDP Even as Iran War Slows Growth
item.description: Italy’s government expects the country’s budget deficit to fall below the European Union limit this year despite a downward revision of its growth forecast due to the Iran war, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Temasek-Backed CuspAI in Talks to Boost Value Above $1 Billion
item.description: CuspAI, a British startup that aims to use artificial intelligence for discovering new materials, is in discussions to raise at least $200 million in new funding, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fitch Sees Mozambique Restructure Its Debt Before New IMF Deal
item.description: Fitch Ratings sees the possibility of a restructuring of Mozambique’s debt likely ahead of any new deal being secured with the International Monetary Fund.

item.title: Cleveland-Cliffs ‘No Longer in a Hurry’ on POSCO Deal, CEO Says
item.description: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc.’s chief executive officer said improving US steel prices and stronger demand from the American auto industry are taking the pressure off closing a deal with South Korea’s POSCO Holdings Inc.

item.title: China Robot’s 50-Minute Half Marathon Shows Pace of AI Progress
item.description: On Sunday morning in a Beijing suburb, a red, humanoid robot completed a half-marathon in 50 minutes and 26 seconds — about seven minutes faster than the men’s world record.

item.title: EQB Inc. Looks to Sell C$200M Limited Recourse Capital Notes
item.description: Canada’s EQB Inc., a bank holding company, is preparing to sell around C$200 million ($146 million) of additional tier one securities as soon as Monday, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: European Oil Refiners See Record Weekly Gain for Gasoline Margin
item.description: Gasoline’s premium to crude posted its biggest weekly gain on record, a boost for refiners that have been under pressure from high oil prices stoked by the Iran war.

item.title: Agnico Pushes Into Finland With C$3.7 Billion Trio of Gold Deals
item.description: Agnico Eagle Mines Ltd. plans to spend C$3.7 billion ($2.7 billion) to acquire three gold projects in northern Finland, expanding the Canadian miner’s footprint in the Scandinavian country.

item.title: ECB’s Pereira Says Economic Damage of Iran War Has Yet to Show
item.description: The Iran war is still too fresh to grasp its full impact on the euro-zone economy, according to European Central Bank Governing Council member Alvaro Santos Pereira.

item.title: Citadel Securities Pitches Lower Tick-Size Pilot Program to SEC
item.description: Citadel Securities asked the Securities and Exchange Commission to test reducing the price increments that some stocks and ETFs trade at before embarking on major market structure changes.

item.title: Citadel Saw Opportunity in Distillate Cracks Ahead of Iran War
item.description: Distillate crack spreads were one area where Citadel saw an opportunity ahead of the Iran war, the financial giant’s head of commodities Sebastian Barrack said at an FT event in London.

item.title: Colombia Kicks Off Global Bond Buyback Ahead of Elections
item.description: Colombia is buying back some of its outstanding global bonds, its third such operation in the past year, as part of a broader strategy to reduce borrowing costs weeks ahead of a tight presidential race.

item.title: Banks Rejected By Supreme Court in Muni Bond Price-Fixing Case
item.description: The US Supreme Court refused to block a class action lawsuit that seeks billions of dollars from Bank of America Corp., JPMorgan Chase & Co. and other financial institutions for allegedly fixing prices in the municipal bond market.

item.title: Tariff Angst Resurfaces Ahead of Earnings From Industrial Giants
item.description: Makers of power equipment, heavy machinery and air conditioners have been among the big winners in the US stock market this year, but renewed tariff concerns put them at risk of stumbling as earnings season gets underway.

item.title: AI Junk-Debt Offering Wave Rolls On as Edged Compute Sells Bonds
item.description: Data center developers are returning to the junk-debt market to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure, adding to wave of new issuance.

item.title: Magyar Unveils Cabinet Team to Lead Hungary’s Economic Reset
item.description: Hungary’s incoming Prime Minister Peter Magyar named the key ministers for his new government who will help shoulder the task of repairing frayed European ties and relaunching a stagnant economy.

item.title: AI Nuclear Power Developer Fermi Slumps After Abrupt Exit of CEO
item.description: Fermi Inc., a developer of nuclear power for AI data centers, slumped after the sudden departure of co-founder and Chief Executive Officer Toby Neugebauer along with the company’s chief financial officer.

item.title: US Homebuilders Set for Another ‘Lost’ Earnings Season
item.description: For US homebuilders, the Iran war dashed what little optimism they had left for this earnings season.

item.title: Psychedelic Stocks Soar as Trump Signs Order to Ease Access
item.description: Shares of companies involved in psychedelic-related therapies are surging in premarket trading Monday after President Donald Trump signed an executive order to expedite research and access.

item.title: Strategy Buys $2.54 Billion of Bitcoin, Most Since November 2024
item.description: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. bought $2.54 billion in Bitcoin over the previous seven days, marking the digital asset treasury company’s largest acquisition of the original cryptocurrency since November 2024.

item.title: Kuwait Declares Force Majeure on Oil Shipments on Hormuz Halt
item.description: Kuwait declared force majeure on shipments of crude oil and refined products as the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz makes it impossible to meet obligations to customers that can’t bring vessels into the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Canada Inflation Jumps to 2.4% as Iran War Drives Up Gas Prices
item.description: Canada’s inflation rate surged to 2.4% in March as the Iran war triggered a record increase in gasoline prices, though it came in slightly lower than economists expected.

item.title: Eni, Repsol Reach Deal to Start Exporting Venezuelan Gas in 2031
item.description: Eni SpA and Repsol SA plan to start exporting natural gas from Venezuela by the end of 2031 after reaching a deal with Caracas to revive a long-stalled effort to expand production from a massive offshore field.
